trust.source: the checked-out repository and its exact package-lock.json dependencies
trust.warning: Source launch executes reviewed repository code and locked dependency lifecycle scripts. It is a local development lane, not an installer.

audit.sideEffect: writes a content-blind audit record only when --write is explicitly supplied

launch.sideEffect: reinstalls exact locked dependencies when either dependency proof is missing or drifted, installs the checksum-verified Electron 43.1.1 native artifact when missing, builds the companion workspace, and starts the native Electron source application

stop.sideEffect: stops only the identity-verified repository launcher process tree

windows: Windows 10 or newer with the Electron-supported desktop runtime
macos: a local macOS graphical login session
linux: a local X11 or Wayland graphical session
a usable Chromium user-namespace sandbox or a correctly configured root-owned 4755 Electron chrome-sandbox helper

allowedByScripts: read repository manifests and fixed build artifacts
check exact Node.js and npm prerequisites
install exact package-lock.json dependencies into this repository when dependency proof is missing or drifted
download and extract the official Electron 43.1.1 native artifact only after the locked installer package and checksums are byte-verified
build @tokenmonster/companion and its local workspace dependencies
start the repository's native Electron source application
stop only the identity-verified repository launcher process tree
requiresSeparateExplicitApproval: modify PATH, shell profiles or operating-system security settings
run an elevated command or host package manager
deniedBySkill: implicit execution when the repository is opened
invoking or installing Codex, Claude Code or any other coding agent
packaging, installer generation, signing, release creation or publishing
reading provider credentials, cookies, secret stores or environment values
uploading lifecycle state, logs or audits
persisting child output, source filenames, project paths or environment values

repositoryLocal.when: either locked dependency proof is missing or drifted, or the checksum-verified Electron native artifact is missing
repositoryLocal.removal: manual after all repository processes stop

repositoryLocal.when: the companion source application is built
repositoryLocal.removal: generated and recreated by the next build

repositoryLocal.when: required local workspace dependencies are built
repositoryLocal.removal: generated and recreated by the next build

repositoryLocal.when: agent lifecycle commands write local state
repositoryLocal.removal: manual after the identity-verified source application stops

userCaches: npm may populate its existing content-addressed cache
the locked Electron installer may cache the official checksum-verified Electron 43.1.1 archive
applicationData: the running source application uses TokenMonster's normal local application data and secret-store boundaries

exitCodes.1: prerequisite, build, runtime or operational failure
exitCodes.2: invalid command usage or contract data

privacy.childOutput: discarded; on Windows readiness uses an ephemeral policy-generated local named pipe with a strict bounded capability-authenticated HELLO-to-READY exchange; the pipe identifier, capability and raw bytes are never logged or persisted; this does not claim same-user-only access or remote-client rejection, and the existing local trust boundary is the same OS login; other platforms accept only the exact readiness marker on stdout
privacy.persistentState: content-blind; no absolute paths, environment values, source filenames or raw child output
privacy.credentials: never read by agent lifecycle scripts
